It is essential to understand that while parts of our identity can strengthen us, they can also make us feel susceptible, especially in situations where societal norms or biases come into play. By sharing our experiences and perspectives, we can enrich conversations about identity, leading to increased empathy, connection, and mutual respect within our communities.
